title: Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic
subtitle: If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.
caption:
Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic, Dec 31, 2022
If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.

This choropleth map displays the strictness of stay-at-home requirements across the globe on December 31, 2022, using a color scale that ranges from no measures to required confinement with few exceptions. The vast majority of the world’s landmass and population falls into the darkest blue category, indicating that stay-at-home orders were required with few exceptions. This pattern covers nearly all of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, as well as the entirety of Africa, Russia, China, and most of Europe, including France, Germany, and the United Kingdom. In North America, Canada and Mexico also appear in this highest restriction band. A lighter shade of blue, representing recommended stay-at-home measures rather than required ones, is visible in the United States, Greenland, and parts of Southeast Asia such as Thailand and Vietnam. The map shows a distinct cluster of orange in South Asia, where India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are colored to show that stay-at-home orders were required except for essential activities. Very few countries appear in the lightest blue band indicating no measures, though some small island nations and territories may fall into this group or the gray "no data" category. The subtitle clarifies that for countries with regional variations, the color represents the strictest sub-region’s policy. The global distribution on this date shows that mandatory stay-at-home rules with minimal exceptions remained the dominant policy approach across continents, with only a handful of nations adopting less stringent recommendations or exemptions for essential workers.
